name: Collado de Hoz Loop
name_original: Puente el Arrudo,  Mirador del Salmón, Linares – ruta circular
description: Cycle the difficult 30.4 miles (48.9 km) Collado de Hoz Loop, gaining 3443 feet (1049 metres) through Cantabria's scenic green hills.
region_intro: Road cycling around Herrerías, located in Cantabria, Spain, features a diverse landscape of mountainous and coastal relief. The region includes abrupt cliffs, river estuaries, and beaches along the coast, transitioning inland to valleys and mountains. This area is primarily composed of limestone with karst topography, contributing to its varied terrain. The humid oceanic climate supports lush, green environments, providing a scenic backdrop for road cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Herrerías The most popular road cycling route is Collado de Hoz Loop, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through green hills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Soplao and Oyambre Park Loop, a difficult 29.1 miles (46.8 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes through Oyambre Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Views of the Zapedo marsh – Oyambre Natural Park loop from Pesués, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through Zapedo marsh and Oyambre Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Herrerías is defined by mountainous terrain, coastal views, and green valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Herrerías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Herrerías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Herrerías?
A: Herrerías offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 70 distinct tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, allowing every cyclist to find a suitable adventure.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Herrerías?
A: The terrain around Herrerías is incredibly diverse, characterized by a mix of mountainous and coastal relief. You'll encounter dramatic cliffs, river estuaries, and beaches along the coast, transitioning inland to green valleys and limestone mountains with karst topography. This varied landscape provides both challenging climbs and scenic coastal rides.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Herrerías is known for its challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123905/collado-de-hoz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Hoz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.4-mile route with over 3,400 feet of elevation gain through scenic green hills. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004536135/el-soplao-and-oyambre-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Soplao and Oyambre Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 29.1-mile ride with over 3,200 feet of climbing, offering stunning coastal views.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Cades&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/861319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes often feature natural parks like Parque Natural de Oyambre, and you might even pass through charming towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Herrerías?
A: While many routes in Herrerías feature significant climbs, there are also easier options suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The region offers a selection of easy and moderate routes, ensuring that cyclists of varying abilities can enjoy the beautiful landscapes. We recommend checking the difficulty ratings for specific routes to find the best fit for your family.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Herrerías?
A: Herrerías experiences a humid oceanic climate with warm summers and mild winters. The lush, green environments are beautiful year-round, but the warmer months, typically from late spring to early autumn, offer the most comfortable conditions for road cycling. During these times, you can expect pleasant temperatures and less precipitation, ideal for enjoying the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Herrerías are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123905/collado-de-hoz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Hoz Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004536135/el-soplao-and-oyambre-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Soplao and Oyambre Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer comprehensive tours of the region's highlights.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Herrerías?
A: The road cycling experience in Herrerías is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's varied terrain, which combines challenging climbs with stunning coastal views and lush green valleys. The well-maintained roads and diverse landscapes are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through natural parks?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to beautiful natural parks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004536135/el-soplao-and-oyambre-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Soplao and Oyambre Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24636667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Zapedo marsh – Oyambre Natural Park loop from Pesués&lt;/a&gt; both take you through the scenic Parque Natural de Oyambre, offering stunning natural scenery and coastal views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views?
A: Many routes in Herrerías provide spectacular coastal views, as the region features a mountainous and coastal relief with abrupt cliffs and beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004536135/el-soplao-and-oyambre-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Soplao and Oyambre Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive coastal vistas as it passes through Oyambre Natural Park.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Herrerías?
A: The roads in the Herrerías area are generally well-maintained, contributing to its appeal for cyclists. While some routes may include unpaved or more challenging segments suitable for experienced riders, the main road cycling paths offer good surfaces for an enjoyable ride through the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Herrerías and its surroundings offer cultural and historical attractions. The village of Herrerías itself has historic forges. Nearby, you can visit towns like Comillas, known for its historic buildings, or San Vicente de la Barquera. Other sites in the broader area include El Capricho de Gaudí, a unique architectural work.
tips: There is a 300-kilogram bronze sculpture depicting a salmon on a stone mass. The work is by the sculptor Jesús Otero (1908-1994).

The section between Linares and La Hermida will be a great surprise. It is a winding mountain road that will leave you with tired legs and a feeling of joy at being able to enjoy such unparalleled views.

On the way down, I suggest you visit the Romanesque church of Santa Juliana, right at the entrance to the town of Lafuente.
Great road that runs through the Cades gorge as part of the Camino Lebaniego. Little traffic.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Collado de Hoz Loop?
faqs.answer: The Collado de Hoz Loop is open and enjoyable throughout the entire year. However, for optimal road cycling conditions, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather with moderate temperatures.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road surface like on this route?
faqs.answer: The Collado de Hoz Loop features an excellent road surface with minimal traffic, making it ideal for racebike cycling. You'll encounter typically rolling Cantabrian hills, with a good mix of gradients. While the main climb is well-paved, be aware that some longer routes incorporating Collado de Hoz might include short unpaved segments.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Collado de Hoz Loop for cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop is generally considered a difficult route, suitable for intermediate to experienced cyclists. It's challenging enough to test your fitness without being overly difficult, offering a good mix of steep and more manageable sections. The total elevation gain is approximately 1049 meters over nearly 49 kilometers.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several picturesque spots. You'll pass by the Salmon Sculpture Viewpoint, and experience the scenic Linares to La Hermida Mountain Road. You'll also ride through the impressive Desfiladero de Cades. From the La Hermida side, the lower slopes provide stunning views across the valley towards the Picos de Europa National Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: While the specific trailhead parking for this exact loop isn't detailed, common starting points for the Collado de Hoz climb with available parking include Sobrelapeña, La Hermida, or Puentenansa. These villages offer amenities and are good bases for starting your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Collado de Hoz Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a dedicated racebike route primarily on paved roads, it is generally not considered suitable or safe for dogs, even on a leash. For dog-friendly outdoor activities, it's best to look for hiking or gravel trails in the region.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Collado de Hoz Loop. It's a public road accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in nearby villages for cyclists?
faqs.answer: Villages like La Hermida, Sobrelapeña, and Puentenansa, which are common starting points for routes incorporating Collado de Hoz, typically offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. These are great places to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Collado de Hoz Loop intersects with or runs alongside several other routes. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Camino Lebaniego (30%), [CIMA CA16] Collado de Hoz * La Hermida (21%), and Transcantábrica (17%). You'll also find smaller segments of [CIMA CA16] Collado de Hoz * Sobrelapeña (13%) and Senda del Deva (6%).

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of Collado de Hoz in cycling?
faqs.answer: The Collado de Hoz gained significant fame in cycling circles for its role in the 2012 Vuelta a España. It was on this climb that Alberto Contador launched a decisive long-range attack, ultimately winning the stage and gaining crucial time that contributed to his overall victory in the race.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting points of the loop?
faqs.answer: While specific public transport details directly to the Collado de Hoz pass are limited, regional bus services might connect to nearby towns like La Hermida or Puentenansa. It's advisable to check local Cantabrian transport schedules for the most up-to-date information if you plan to use public transport with your bike.

waypoints.name: Linares to La Hermida Mountain Road
